that our author refers here to a northern settlement of the Mala-
vas who lived between the lower Ravi and the Chenab in the 4th
century B.C.
The Pauravas or Purus lived on the eastern bank of the Jhelum
including the Gujarat district. In the 4th century B.C., when
Alexander invaded India, Porus, probably the PQru chief, was ruling
over the region between the rivers Jhelum and Chenab. King
Du§yanta is said to be a descendant of Puru.
Hums are often mentioned by classical writers such as Kali-
dasa and Bana. Prabhakaravardhana is styled 'Lion to the Huna
deer'. Skandagupta had to encounter the Hupas. Yuan Chwang
says that Baladitya, son of Tathagatagupta, imprisoned and later
released the Huna chief Mihirakula. Having been expelled from
central India, the Htmas seem to have confined themselves to Kash-
mir and Gandhara.
Gnndhnra extended from (he Kabul valley to Tak§asila and
Comprised the Rawalpindi and Peshawar districts.
